title: Rothia andryaloides
intro: Rothia andryaloides, also known as Rothia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Capparaceae. Traditionally, Rothia has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rothia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Rothia is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies such as decoctions and ointments.
